titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5963

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=770 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 21:10:50.701297+00 | translation moa=771 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 21:10:50.709885+00

p.cl:
. Omnibus hoc scriptum visuris uel Audituris Willelmus filius Willelmi de scremerstona
salutem.

me ratum et gratum habuisse et hac presenti car
ta mea confirmasse Deo et sancto Cvhtberto priori et monachiis de Coldingham
Ibidem deo seruientibus et in perpetuum seruituris Donacionem et concessionem Totius
terre quam Willelmus de scremerstona pater meus habuit In villa et territorio de pren
dergest’ ex dono Roberti fili Roberti de prendergest sine alico retinemento
videlicet sex bouatas terre cum pertinentiis In territorio de prendergest illas sili
cet Duas bouatas terre cum Tofto et Crofto quas Robertus Wynd tenuit. et illas
Duas bouatas terre quas Willelmus filius Galfridi tenuit. et Illas Duas bouatas
terre quas Galfridus filius Waldeui tenuit. et Toftum et Croftum que aldanus
filius Waldeui tenuit. et Toftum et Croftum que Ricardus filius angnetis
tenuit. Quas dictas terras cum Domibus. Toftis et Croftis et cum omnibus suis
pertinentiis habuit ex dono Roberti fili Roberti de prendergest In villa et
territorio de prendergest’. Habendum et Tenendum dictis priori et conuentui de
Coldingham et eorum successoribus inperpetuum adeo libere. quiete et honorifice In
omnibus sicut carta predicti Willelmi patris mei prefatis priori et Conuentui de
Coldingham de prioratis terris cum omnibus pertinentiis confecta plenius
et melius testatur. Et ego Willelmus filius Willelmi de scremerstona et heredes mei
prescriptam donacionem et Concessionem omnium predictarum terrarum de prendergest’
cum omnibus suis pertinentiis infra villam et extra sepedictis priori et monachis
de Coldingham et eorum successoribus contra omnes homines et feminas in perpetuum
warantizabimus. adquietabimus. et defendemus.

In Cuius rei Testimonium
huic scripto sigillum meum appossui.

Hiis Testibus domino Willelmo de lindesei
domino Willelmo de Mordingtona . domino Alano de swintona. alano filio suo. Thoma
de Nesbyt
Ada filio suo. Roberto filio Gregorii de Coldingham. Dauid de lumes
den. Gilberto de lumesden. Ada filio Iohannis de Eytona. Bertramo de Ristona. Iohanne
filio Ingerami. Roberto filio Reginaldi. patricio scoto. et ALIIS.

div.p: To all who shall view or hear this writing, William, son of William of Scremerston, sends greeting. Know me, all of you, to have ratified and made firm by this my present charter, to God and to Saint Cuthbert, to the prior and monks of Coldingham serving God in that place now and in the future, for ever, the donation and grant of the whole of the land which my father, William of Scremerston, had in the toun and territory of Prenderguest out of the gift of Robert son of Robert of Prenderguest, without any withholding; namely, six oxgangs of land with pertinents in the territory of Prenderguest; that is, two oxgangs of land with the toft and croft which Robert Wynd held; and the two oxgangs which William son of Geoffrey held; and the two oxgangs of land which Geoffrey son of Waldef held; and the toft and croft which Haldan son of Waldef held; and the toft and croft which Richard son of Agnes held. These said lands, with dwellings, tofts, and crofts, and with all their pertinents, I had out of the gift of Robert, son of Robert of Prenderguest, in the toun and territory of Prenderguest. For the said prior and convent of Coldingham and their successors to hold and to have for ever, as freely, quitely, and honourably in all things as the charter of the aforesaid William, my father, drawn up for the prior and convent of Coldingham concerning the lands of the priory with all pertinents, fully and well bears witness. And I, William, son of William of Scremerston, and my heirs, shall warrant, acquit, and defend the aforewritten donation and grant of all the aforesaid lands of Prenderguest with all their pertinents, within the toun and outside, for the oft-mentioned prior and monks of Coldingham and their successors against all men and women for ever. In testimony of this matter, I have applied my seal to this writing. With these witnesses: the lord William of Lindsay, the lord William of Mordington, the lord Alan of Swinton, Alan his son, Thomas of Nisbet, Adam his son, Robert son of Gregory of Coldingham, David of Lumesden, Gilbert of Lumesden, Adam son of John of Ayton, Bertram of Reston, John son of Ingram, Robert son of Reginald, Patrick Scott, and others.
